title: NOC 22111 – Agricultural and Fish Products Inspectors | GTR Immigration
description: Agricultural and Fish Products Inspectors are in growing demand across Canada, driven by the expansion of food-processing facilities, seafood exports, agricultural certification systems, and federal/provincial food-safety regulations. Classified under NOC 22111 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Inspect fish, meat, grain, crops, and produce for quality & safety
Grade grain shipments and oversee fumigation
Certify seed crops and plant exports
employment_requirements: College diploma or bachelor’s degree in agriculture, biology, chemistry, or food science
Several years of experience in farming, fisheries, or food-processing operations
Government roles may require higher academic credentials
job_outlook: This balanced outlook makes the occupation attractive for candidates with:
additional_information: Food-safety modernization and export certification rules have made inspectors essential in:
Food-manufacturing plantsSeafood-processing facilitiesGrain terminals & elevatorsMeat-processing operationsPlant-health inspection agenciesGovernment regulatory departments
Annual salaries typically range between $34,700–$82,600 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and experience level.
